# Cronograma Creator — AI Agent Instructions

> Version: 4.1
> App URL: https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/
> Share endpoint: https://odnysrjhbxwjnwrsrqpw.supabase.co/functions/v1/create-share
> State schema: https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/state.schema.json
> Well-known manifest: https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/.well-known/cronograma-ai.json

Cronograma Creator is an interactive Gantt chart builder. AI agents can build a schedule and hand the user a short share URL with **one HTTP request** — no browser, no auth, no client SDK required.

---

## QUICK START — One HTTP request, one short URL

POST the cronograma state to the public share endpoint. You get back a short URL the user can open immediately.

```http
POST https://odnysrjhbxwjnwrsrqpw.supabase.co/functions/v1/create-share
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "title": "Q2 Roadmap",
  "state": {
    "title": "Q2 Roadmap",
    "startMonth": "0",
    "endMonth": "5",
    "theme": "claro",
    "lang": "es",
    "projects": [
      {
        "name": "Diseño",
        "color": "#5B8CC8",
        "tasksByRow": [
          [{ "name": "Research",   "startWeek": 1, "duration": 3 }],
          [{ "name": "Wireframes", "startWeek": 4, "duration": 4 }]
        ]
      },
      {
        "name": "Desarrollo",
        "color": "#2E9E58",
        "tasksByRow": [
          [{ "name": "Backend",  "startWeek": 5,  "duration": 8 }],
          [{ "name": "Frontend", "startWeek": 9,  "duration": 6 }],
          [{ "name": "Launch",   "startWeek": 15, "duration": 1, "isMilestone": true }]
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

**Response (200 OK):**
```json
{
  "id": "aB3xK9mP2q",
  "url": "https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/?c=aB3xK9mP2q"
}
```

Give the user the `url`. Opening it loads the cronograma instantly in any browser.

### Language examples

**curl:**
```bash
curl -X POST https://odnysrjhbxwjnwrsrqpw.supabase.co/functions/v1/create-share \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"title":"My Plan","state":{ ... }}'
```

**JavaScript / Node:**
```javascript
const res = await fetch(
  'https://odnysrjhbxwjnwrsrqpw.supabase.co/functions/v1/create-share',
  {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: { 'content-type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify({ title: 'My Plan', state })
  }
);
const { url } = await res.json();
console.log(url);
```

**Python:**
```python
import requests
r = requests.post(
    'https://odnysrjhbxwjnwrsrqpw.supabase.co/functions/v1/create-share',
    json={'title': 'My Plan', 'state': state},
    timeout=10,
)
print(r.json()['url'])
```

---

## ENDPOINT REFERENCE

### `POST /functions/v1/create-share`

| Header | Required | Value |
|---|---|---|
| `Content-Type` | yes | `application/json` |

| Body field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `state` | object | yes | Full cronograma state — see schema below |
| `title` | string | no | Title shown in UI. Defaults to `state.title` or `"Cronograma"`. Max 200 chars |

| Response | Body |
|---|---|
| `200 OK` | `{ "id": "<short-id>", "url": "<full URL>" }` |
| `400 Bad Request` | `{ "error": "<reason>" }` — invalid JSON or invalid state |
| `413 Payload Too Large` | state JSON > 180 KB |
| `429 Too Many Requests` | rate limit: max 30 shares per IP per hour |

**Lifecycle:** Anonymous shares expire 90 days after creation. No auth required to create or read.

---

## STATE SCHEMA

Canonical JSON Schema: [`state.schema.json`](state.schema.json)

### Top-level fields

| Field | Type | Required | Values |
|---|---|---|---|
| `title` | string | yes | Any text |
| `startMonth` | string or int | yes | `"0"`–`"11"` (`0` = January) |
| `endMonth` | string or int | yes | `"0"`–`"11"`, must be ≥ `startMonth` |
| `theme` | string | no | `"claro"` `"oscuro"` `"moderno"` `"gris"` |
| `lang` | string | no | `"es"` `"en"` |
| `projects` | array | yes | At least 1, max 100 — see below |

### Project fields

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | yes | Group label |
| `color` | string | yes | Hex color e.g. `"#5B8CC8"` |
| `compact` | boolean | no | Smaller row height for the whole project |
| `tasksByRow` | array of arrays | yes | Each inner array = one visual row (tasks in same row may overlap) |

### Task fields

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | yes | Task label |
| `startWeek` | integer ≥ 1 | yes | `1` = first week of `startMonth` |
| `duration` | integer ≥ 1 | yes | Duration in weeks |
| `isMilestone` | boolean | no | Diamond shape; use `duration: 1` |
| `textPosition` | string | no | `"inside"` or `"outside"` |
| `compact` | boolean | no | Smaller row height for this task |
| `completed` | boolean | no | Strikethrough style |
| `color` | string \| null | no | Hex override; `null` = inherit project color |
| `description` | string | no | Notes shown in the edit modal, max 500 chars |

### Default color palette (cycle order)
`#6C8EBF` `#9B7EB8` `#D4915E` `#6BAF7B` `#C4A95B` `#5BA8A0` `#7EA3C9` `#B07DA5` `#C4956E` `#8BB5A2`

---

## LEGACY: stateless hash URL (still supported)

For environments where you can't make an HTTP request, the original method still works — encode the state as base64 and append it to the URL hash:

```javascript
const hash = btoa(unescape(encodeURIComponent(JSON.stringify(state))));
const url = `https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/#s=${hash}`;
```

```python
import json, base64
encoded = base64.b64encode(json.dumps(state, ensure_ascii=False).encode('utf-8')).decode('utf-8')
url = f"https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/#s={encoded}"
```

Tradeoff: URLs become very long for non-trivial cronogramas. Prefer the POST endpoint above.

---

## BROWSER API (optional)

When the page is already open in a browser, `window.CronogramaAPI` is available:

```javascript
CronogramaAPI.createFromJSON(state);           // load a state
CronogramaAPI.getState();                      // read current state
const url = CronogramaAPI.getShareUrl();       // get the legacy #s= URL
const png = await CronogramaAPI.getImageDataUrl(); // PNG as data URL
const { valid, errors } = CronogramaAPI.validateState(state);
```

### Headless workflows (Playwright / Puppeteer)

```javascript
// Option A: open a short share URL
await page.goto('https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/?c=<short-id>');

// Option B: open with mode=agent and inject state directly
await page.goto('https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/?mode=agent');
await page.evaluate((s) => CronogramaAPI.createFromJSON(s), state);
const png = await page.evaluate(() => CronogramaAPI.getImageDataUrl());
```

---

## URL PARAMETERS

All parameters can be combined.

| Param | Effect |
|---|---|
| `?c=<short-id>` | Load a publicly-shared cronograma by its short ID |
| `#s=<base64>` | Load a cronograma encoded directly in the hash (legacy) |
| `?mode=agent` | Agent mode: suppresses confirms, deterministic render, console logs prefixed `[CronogramaAI]` |
| `?autoexport=url` | On load, writes `{success, type, data}` to `#autoexport-result` with the share URL |
| `?autoexport=image` | On load, writes `{success, type, data}` with a PNG data URL |
| `?autoexport=json` | On load, writes the full state JSON |

---

## DOM HOOKS FOR TESTING

After every render:

```javascript
document.getElementById('app-test-state').dataset.shareUrl   // legacy share URL
document.getElementById('app-test-state').dataset.state      // full state as JSON string
document.getElementById('app-test-state').dataset.imageReady // "true" after first render
```

When using `?autoexport=`:

```javascript
JSON.parse(document.getElementById('autoexport-result').dataset.result);
// { success: true, type: "url" | "image" | "json", data: ... }
```

---

## DISCOVERY FILES

For agents, crawlers, and scanners that probe well-known paths, the following discovery documents are published alongside this file:

| Path | Purpose |
|---|---|
| [`/llms.txt`](https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/llms.txt) | This document. Primary agent-readable spec. |
| [`/robots.txt`](https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/robots.txt) | Crawler policy, including AI-bot rules and Cloudflare-style Content-Signal (`search=yes, ai-input=yes, ai-train=no`). |
| [`/sitemap.xml`](https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/sitemap.xml) | Sitemap covering the app, `llms.txt`, and `state.schema.json`. |
| [`/state.schema.json`](https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/state.schema.json) | JSON Schema (draft-07) for the cronograma state object. |
| [`/.well-known/cronograma-ai.json`](https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/.well-known/cronograma-ai.json) | Top-level AI manifest. Lists capabilities, URL params, browser API and the share endpoint. |
| [`/.well-known/agent-skills.json`](https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/.well-known/agent-skills.json) | Structured skills agents can perform (`create_timeline`, `validate_timeline_state`, `export_share_url`, `export_png`, `export_json`, `load_timeline`, `self_test`) with input/output schemas. |
| [`/.well-known/api-catalog.json`](https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/.well-known/api-catalog.json) | Catalog of all callable APIs: the public HTTP share endpoint and the browser-side `window.CronogramaAPI` surface. |
| [`/.well-known/mcp-server.json`](https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/.well-known/mcp-server.json) | Explicit declaration that no remote MCP server is hosted. Points scanners to the HTTP endpoint and browser JS API instead. |
| [`/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server`](https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server) | Explicit declaration that no OAuth authorization server is exposed. |
| [`/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource`](https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource) | Explicit declaration that no resource here is OAuth-protected; all public endpoints listed. |

**Note on content negotiation:** GitHub Pages does not perform content negotiation. There is no HTML-vs-JSON variant of any resource — each URL above returns exactly the file at that path. `llms.txt` is the canonical agent-readable representation; the JSON manifests under `.well-known/` are machine-readable summaries cross-referencing this file.

---

## NOTES

- `startWeek` is 1-based. Week 1 = first Monday of `startMonth`.
- Tasks in the same inner array of `tasksByRow` share a horizontal row and may overlap visually.
- `color: null` on a task means it inherits the project color.
- The app auto-saves to `localStorage` and (when signed in) to the user's Supabase account. Use `?mode=agent` to avoid local side-effects.
- Public shares (`?c=`) are read-only snapshots — opening one in a logged-in account effectively forks it as a new draft.
